notes: Suppresses respiratory-distress call-type rubric items already owned by the universal NREMT base rubric in this training scenario. These items remain in the NASEMSO respiratory distress rubric for standalone QA/QI use but must not double-score or produce false misses when the base patient-care rubric is active.

operations.reason: Universal NREMT base rubric owns PPE and scene safety via scene_entry evidence path. The respiratory-distress call-type item duplicates the same base expectation and can create a second Call Specific row for a universal assessment step.
operations.protocol_ref: NREMT EMT Patient Assessment/Management Medical E202 — scene safety and PPE are base rubric items

operations.reason: Universal NREMT base rubric owns general impression. The respiratory-distress call-type item is redundant in this training scenario and should not double-score the same scene-entry/general-impression action.
operations.protocol_ref: NREMT EMT Patient Assessment/Management Medical E202 — general impression is a base rubric item
